## Section 2: What Resonated

### 1. deepwiki.com as a bridge for non-technical understanding
- **Quote:** "Even I didn't realize the system architecture was this elaborate."
- **Why it resonated:** V shared deepwiki.com (turns GitHub repos into navigable wikis) and David immediately saw it as a way to understand N5OS without reading raw code. For a non-technical person, this was a revelation — it made V's system legible.
- **Signal/Implication:** David's primary blocker is comprehension, not motivation. Tools that lower the technical barrier accelerate his activation.

### 2. The AI messaging system concept — encoding David's methodology
- **Quote:** "Where I see people end up needing the most help after they learn from me, is getting stuck on what to message."
- **Why it resonated:** This is the core of David's product vision — he can see the exact workflow (candidate background + target LinkedIn + his principles → personalized outreach), has validated the pain point through cohorts, and wants AI to scale it.
- **Signal/Implication:** This isn't theoretical — David has paying students hitting this exact wall. The demand signal is live and tested.

### 3. Zo as the right platform for his vision (persistence over ephemeral)
- **Quote:** "That's why I was liking the idea of Zo, because... that's what I was trying to also — so there's like two conversations going on here. One is, how does David build his thing? Can it be with Zo?"
- **Why it resonated:** David explicitly named his dual interest — building his product AND helping Zo articulate its value. He's trying to solve both simultaneously.
- **Signal/Implication:** David sees himself as both user and evangelist. This is the power-user archetype Zo needs — someone building a real product on the platform.

### 4. Zo's messaging problem mirrors Careerspan's
- **Quote (V):** "They have a Careerspan-esque problem in that they don't know how to communicate the value of something that sounds sort of nebulous."
- **David's response:** "You need to find the people who are just smart enough to use projects and custom GPTs... they realize they're limited by dumping a small subset of files into that project and getting stuck."
- **Why it resonated:** David immediately articulated Zo's ideal customer profile better than most — the "smart enough to hit the ceiling" user. Referenced Noah King's post as proof.
- **Signal/Implication:** David has genuine product marketing instincts for Zo's positioning, not just networking value.

### 5. Cohort progress — first revenue milestone
- **Quote:** "I got four paid students... one of them paid retail at 17.95 and didn't even show up."
- **Why it resonated:** V's excitement was genuine ("Dude, that's sick, man"). David has crossed from planning to revenue.
- **Signal/Implication:** David is building a real business, not just exploring. This gives his AI messaging system concept commercial urgency.

By Jan 2026, he has shifted to active Zo user trying to build his own product on the platform. This is a fundamental role evolution from connector → builder.
